rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Respondents/Claimants Glenn E. Stamps And Allen J. Stamps, Individually And In Their Capacity As Executors Of The Estate Of Allen Stamps, Jr.
rulings.Rulings: This tentative is issued by Hon. Joseph M. Quinn The Court GRANTS Beneficiary and Moving Party Juanita Lewis's unopposed Request for Judicial Notice. The Court GRANTS Respondents/Claimants Glenn E. Stamps and Allen J. Stamps' unopposed Request for Judicial Notice. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) By the pending Petition for Determination that Petitioner Juanita Lewis Is the Person Entitled to Distribution of the Remaining Estate ("Section 11700 Petition"), Juanita Lewis seeks an order declaring her the sole beneficiary of the estate of her sister, Josephine Stamps. Such an order would entitle Lewis to what's left of the proceeds from the sale of 1216 Fulton St. Glenn and Allen Stamps, who are the children of Josephine's stepson Allen Stamps, Jr., respond that Lewis is not the only person entitled to distribution and, in fact, they're entitled to 50% of the proceeds from the sale of the residence. Lewis moves for Judgment on the Pleadings, seeking judgment against the Stamps' response and claim. Lewis's motion is DENIED. Preliminarily, the court rejects the Stamps' claim that Lewis's motion is improper. Where it is plain on the face of the pleadings that a Section 11700 claimant has no colorable claim as a beneficiary of the estate or a person otherwise entitled to distribution of a share of the estate surely the court can adjudicate that claim without holding an evidentiary hearing. (See Prob. C., section 11700.) The Stamps have not demonstrated that a motion for judgment on the pleadings is not a proper vehicle for such adjudication. Nor is the court convinced that the motion must be denied because Lewis failed to provide adequate notice. The motion focuses on the Stamps' response and claim and the Stamps have not demonstrated any prejudice. The motion, however, fails on the merits. The Stamps contend that Lewis is not the only person entitled to distribution and that Josephine Stamps' 9/17/1993 will calls for distribution of 50% of the proceeds from the sale of 1216 Fulton Street to them (as Allen Stamps Jr.'s heirs). It is unclear whether the Stamps' theory is that the will is ambiguous but subject to their construction or the will is unambiguous but should be reformed to conform to Josephine's intent. Nonetheless, construing the pleadings liberally, Lewis has not shown that the allegations of, and the information in, the operative pleadings are insufficient to support the Stamps' contentions or the Stamps, as a matter of law, are not entitled to present evidence on their theories. (See, e.g., Estate of Duke (2015) 61 Cal.4th 871.) Additionally, on this record (and assuming the truth of all material allegations), Lewis has failed to demonstrate that the Stamps' claim to a share of Josephine Stamps' estate is untimely. The Stamps are ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on March 11, 2021. Remote appearance is required, whenever feasible, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.
